title: Meta enters the cloud and triggers massive sell-off of chip stocks; Asian markets and Wall Street fall
summary: Meta's plans to sell excess AI capacity and OpenAI's advances cause a wave of selling in the semiconductor sector, affecting stock markets worldwide.

title: ECB rules out short-term rate hikes and inflation falls to 2.8% in the eurozone
summary: Lagarde and other ECB members distance themselves from further rate hikes, while annual inflation declines and Euribor confirms its downward trend.

title: New Fed Chairman Kevin Warsh shakes markets with his monetary ambiguity
summary: Warsh's evasive statements generate uncertainty and losses on Wall Street, as the market digests his plans for monetary policy.

title: Bitcoin falls and EU demands licenses for crypto groups, only a minority obtain them
summary: Bitcoin's price depreciates by 2%, and the new European licensing rules are only complied with by a small fraction of cryptocurrency operators.

title: KNDS postpones its IPO after investor resistance to its valuation
summary: German tank manufacturer KNDS withdraws its initial public offering due to lack of interest in its valuation exceeding €12 billion.

title: Yen rises against dollar, speculation grows of Bank of Japan intervention
summary: The yen's strengthening fuels expectations that Japanese authorities could intervene in the foreign exchange market, while 'Mr. Yen' keeps investors on edge.

title: Dax surpasses 25,000 points and Ibex takes a breather; Wall Street closes best quarter since 2020
summary: European stock markets reach historic milestones, while the S&P 500 completes its strongest quarter in several years.

title: Commerzbank in the crosshairs: ECB pushes for Unicredit takeover and Spain rules it out as debt underwriter
summary: The central bank pressures Unicredit to acquire Commerzbank, while the Spanish Treasury dispenses with its services as a debt placement agent.

title: US labor market re-accelerates and investors await employment data
summary: Wall Street futures remain steady ahead of employment figures, while analysis indicates the US economy could be re-accelerating.

title: Oracle appoints new general manager in Spain, sparking debate among investors
summary: Sergio Sáez takes over as head of Oracle in Spain, while the company's stock is a topic of discussion in investor networks.

title: Northern Star Resources changes CEO and chairman amid pressure from Elliott
summary: The Australian mining company appoints a new CEO and chairman following pressure from activist fund Elliott, and its shares rise.

title: Trump earned more than $1 billion in 2025 and bought $1.4 billion worth of stocks
summary: Donald Trump's financial disclosure reveals massive income and large stock purchases during his return to the presidency.

title: US and Iran hold indirect talks on the Strait of Hormuz
summary: The United States uses intermediary countries to negotiate with Iran on navigation in the Strait of Hormuz, seeking to avoid the imposition of fees.

title: Getty cancels $3.7 billion merger with Shutterstock after UK regulator order
summary: The acquisition of Shutterstock by Getty Images is abandoned after the UK competition authority blocked it.

title: Google ordered to pay $2 billion to Klarna for antitrust damages
summary: A court orders Google to compensate Klarna with $2 billion for antitrust practices in the payments market.

title: Bending Spoons, owner of AOL and Eventbrite, soars 40% in Nasdaq debut
summary: The tech company Bending Spoons goes public with a strong valuation increase, standing out on its first day of trading.

title: Uber-backed Lime rises in stock market after raising $167 million in IPO
summary: Urban mobility company Lime raises $167 million in its initial public offering and its shares rise on debut.

title: US pushes voluntary AI standards and FTC warns about biases
summary: The Trump administration negotiates with tech companies to create voluntary AI standards, while the Federal Trade Commission warns that safeguards against bias may be illegal.

title: World Cup fans sue StubHub over ticket cancellations
summary: A group of football fans files a lawsuit against the resale platform StubHub for the cancellation of tickets for World Cup matches.

title: Kroger buys Giant Eagle for $1.6 billion in supermarket sector consolidation
summary: Supermarket chain Kroger acquires its rival Giant Eagle in a deal valued at $1.6 billion.
